result.chapter_summary: Hirsch argues that the American educational decline, evidenced by low international rankings and falling verbal scores, is the result of an 80-year failed experiment with 'Romantic' child-centered pedagogy. He contends that national renewal requires abandoning the 'religion of nature' in favor of a shared curriculum that imparts the national grapholect, a task made more urgent by recent legal shifts in affirmative action.

claims.text: The optimism of Americans in the 1930s and 40s was rooted in economic growth and successful contributions to World War I and World War II.
claims.source_passage: We were optimistic about the nation’s economic growth and its successful contributions to World War I. That optimism was reinforced later in the 40s by our decisive victory in World War II.

claims.text: The founding ideas of Time magazine were influenced by a confidence in the American future taught in Yale's philosophy and history departments.
claims.source_passage: Time magazine had been started in 1923 by two brilliant young graduates from Yale, who had breathed in confidence in the American future from their courses in the Yale philosophy and history departments.

claims.text: University courses in the early 20th century featured thinkers like John Dewey and Hegel, who promoted a romantic, optimistic view of the future.
claims.source_passage: And some of those courses featured the father of American child-centered education, the philosopher John Dewey, as well as Dewey’s own favorite philosopher, Hegel, who supported his romantic optimistic view of the future.

claims.text: Dewey’s argument that child-centered education prepares children for an unpredictable future is a non sequitur relying on an unspoken trust in Nature.
claims.source_passage: You said that child-centered education would prepare them for that changing future world that you rightly said is unpredictable. That doesn’t follow. But then, maybe it does, once we invoke an unspoken logic: 'You and I may not know where the rapidly changing world is tending, but God knows. And God—through Nature—will guide the child’s instincts'

claims.text: The United States currently possesses the twenty-fifth best elementary school system in the world despite being the richest nation.
claims.source_passage: According to the current Program for International Student Assessment (PISA), the United States, the richest nation in the world, now has the twenty-fifth best elementary school system in the world.

claims.text: Nature is indifferent and does not provide individual children with the necessary instincts for optimal development into society.
claims.source_passage: Nature is quite indifferent. She does not infuse the individual child with just the right instincts and impulses that will guide her optimal “development” into our society and our future era.

claims.text: The 80-year American educational experiment with 'Romantic faith' in child-centered education is a failed experiment.
claims.source_passage: Our 80-year educational experiment with Romantic faith is a failed experiment—as Professor Hayes graphically showed in his chart of our verbal scores.

claims.text: Educational progress requires divesting from the false 'religion of nature' and false technical claims like readability and critical thinking.
claims.source_passage: This book has argued that if we manage to divest ourselves of a false romantic religion of nature, supported by a false set of technical claims (readability, critical thinking) we will move forward in elementary education.

claims.text: The Supreme Court ruling against affirmative action increases the urgency of providing optimal early schooling to overcome disadvantage.
claims.source_passage: I write this on the day (June, 29, 2023) that our Supreme Court has ruled against affirmative action in college admissions for students of color. That makes all the more urgent the need to overcome disadvantage by optimal early schooling.

claims.text: Child-centered schooling is inherently incapable of being optimal because literacy requires shared background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Child-centered schooling emphatically is not and cannot be optimal, given the inherent necessity of shared background knowledge for gaining high literacy and advanced knowledge.

claims.text: Imparting expertise in the national grapholect is the primary duty of a nation’s schools in the modern era.
claims.source_passage: To impart that expertise is the primary duty of a nation’s schools in the modern era. It is the only way that fairness and the optimal use of a young person’s talents can be achieved.

claims.text: The quality of education in grades K-5 is life-determining for disadvantaged children.
claims.source_passage: As the chart showing the “French Elbow” makes clear, the quality of grades K-5 are life-determining grades for disadvantaged children.

claims.text: American elementary education was formerly first-rate when guided by Enlightenment ideas like those of John Locke and the 'common school' model.
claims.source_passage: Our elementary education used to be first-rate under the “common school,” and the guidance of ideas from Enlightenment philosophers like John Locke.

claims.text: American education was overtaken by Romanticism and a false 'religion of Nature' following the French Revolution.
claims.source_passage: But then our education was overtaken by Romanticism and the religion of Nature, which was a false religion that cannot be relied on.

claims.text: Romanticism entered the American consciousness through figures like Emerson, Thoreau, and Whitman before influencing John Dewey.
claims.source_passage: In the 19th Century, “romanticism” infused the United States in figures like Emerson, Thoreau, Walt Whitman and later John Dewey.

claims.text: The religion of Nature is a false religion that cannot be relied upon.
claims.source_passage:
ism and the religion of 
Nature, which was a false religion that cannot be relied on.

claims.text: Enlightenment thinkers viewed revealed religion with suspicion due to the violent sectarianism displayed in the Wars of Religion.
claims.source_passage:
In Europe, even before the French 
Revolution, revealed religion was under a cloud for thinkers of the 
Enlightenment because of the unforgiving murderous absolutism of 
sectarian theology in the Wars of Religion.

claims.text: The Thirty Years War (1618-1648) killed one-third of the population of Germany.
claims.source_passage:
One of those wars—the Thirty 
Years War (1618-1648)—had killed a third of the population of Germany.

claims.text: The American founders wisely insisted upon the separation of church and state.
claims.source_passage: Our founders wisely insisted upon the separation of church and state.

claims.text: The French and American Revolutions emboldened Romantic thinkers to reject priestly dogma and prioritize earthly fulfillment.
claims.source_passage:
The French Revolution (key date: 1789) along with the American Revolution 
(Constitution ratified 1791) emboldened romantic thinkers to look not for 
pie in the sky, but to pie on earth; to reject “priestly dogma” and repression.

claims.text: Romanticism posits that the natural growth of the child should be trusted as it is closer to the divine source than the 'meddling intellect' of adults.
claims.source_passage:
The child’s natural growth was to be trusted. The child was closer to the 
divine source than the grown up whose “meddling intellect” “dissected” 
things rather than instinctively unifying them.

claims.text: Hegel's influence on John Dewey was fateful for American schools, shifting the aim of education from national formation to a divinely-inspired evolution.
claims.source_passage:
Hegel’s influence over John Dewey was fateful for our schools. Hegel’s 
notion of a gradually evolving world spirit marching ever forward in time 
led to an American change of educational thought—from conceiving the 
aim of education as forming the child to be a happy, effective member of a 
particular nation—to education as a God-inspired evolution growing out 
of mankind’s evolving, inherently divine manifestation of Geist.

claims.text: John Dewey's philosophy was an attempt to combine the historical evolution of Hegel with the biological evolution of Darwin.
claims.source_passage:
John Dewey’s philosophy has been well described by Richard Rorty: 
an attempt to combine Hegel with Darwin.35 That’s a lot to chew on, but it 
really is not all that complicated. It means that Dewey thought things are 
evolving positively in human history just as they were doing in Nature.

claims.text: The Hegel/Dewey progressive doctrine conflicts with the 'blank slate' principles of the early American common school.
claims.source_passage:
That quick outline of the Hegel /Dewey “progressive” doctrine conflicts 
with the educational principles of our early “common school” which built 
our nation.

claims.text: The American founders' common school conceived the child as being born with a 'blank slate' (Locke's term) without inborn ideas.
claims.source_passage:
Our founders’ common school conceived the child as being 
born with a “blank slate” (John Locke’s term) without any inborn ideas 
from “nature,” a word that means “inborn.”

claims.text: The Lockean 'blank slate' theory is fundamental to the principles of equality of worth and equality of citizenship.
claims.source_passage:
That was Locke’s insistent view. It was fundamental to the theory of equality of worth, and the equality of 
citizenship, and the principle of giving everyone an equal chance.

claims.text: Hegel's 'Phenomenology of Spirit' proposed that mental development is part of an inevitable theological cosmic progress.
claims.source_passage:
The Hegel book that made its mark on 
Dewey’s thinking was The Phenomenology of Spirit (1807). It proposed a 
story of mental development as part of an inevitable theological cosmic 
progress.

claims.text: Hegel's account of history suggests that human progress is governed by an unstoppable and beneficent 'dialectic' and 'Logik.'
claims.source_passage:
In Hegel’s account of human history, 
one thing leads inexorably to another by a “necessity” that expresses itself 
in a “dialectic” of trial and error and a “Logik” that is both unstoppable 
and beneficent.

claims.text: Hegelian philosophy is essentially a form of rational theology.
claims.source_passage:
Hegel once said: “Philosophy has no other object but God and so is essentially 
rational theology.”

claims.text: Hegel's Phenomenology of Spirit characterizes human history as an unstoppable and beneficent unfolding of 'Geist' or Spirit through a necessary dialectic process.
claims.source_passage: In Hegel’s account of human history, one thing leads inexorably to another by a “necessity” that expresses itself in a “dialectic” of trial and error and a “Logik” that is both unstoppable and beneficent.

claims.text: John Dewey's educational philosophy was fundamentally shaped by Hegel's optimistic theory of inevitable historical progress.
claims.source_passage: Hegel’s book won over John Dewey. . . Hegel’s book encouraged Dewey to believe that “Time Marches On”—and is doing so inexorably—just like Darwin’s view of evolution.

claims.text: The belief in inevitable progress appealed to 20th-century Americans because it aligned with their optimistic national outlook and recent social advances.
claims.source_passage: Hegel’s claim appealed to Dewey and other Americans of the twentieth century because it was an optimistic theory of history and human progress which fit their optimistic view of our nation.

claims.text: Major American social progress, such as abolition and women's equality, was achieved by Enlightenment activists rather than Romantic philosophers.
claims.source_passage: the social progress of our American history was accomplished not by complacent romantics like Hegel and Dewey but by the Enlightenment philosophers and activists who preceded them.

claims.text: Child-centered education is based on a secular religion of natural progress derived from 19th-century Romanticism.
claims.source_passage: Child-centered education has thus been based on a religious doctrine whose historical source was the romantic movement of the nineteenth century.

claims.text: The complacency inherent in 'progressive' child-centered education is a moral and practical weakness.
claims.source_passage: A confidence in “progressive” child-centered education is a too-complacent point of view. And that is its inherent moral and practical weakness.

claims.text: National flourishing requires the 'eternal vigilance' of Enlightenment thinking rather than the 'wise passiveness' of Romanticism.
claims.source_passage: They made America flourish under Enlightenment, activist ideas. They said: “The price of liberty is eternal vigilance,” not eternal complacency. It is not the “wise passiveness” of Wordsworth.

claims.text: Romantic child-centered education is inherently antithetical to the rational and patriotic founding ideas of the United States.
claims.source_passage: It is inherently antithetical to our founding ideas, which, though they came earlier, are sounder and more advanced than complacent romanticism.

claims.text: The Hegel-Darwin-Dewey tradition in education has led to a decline in patriotism, social unity, and educational quality.
claims.source_passage: Child-centered Hegelian confidence has led to unproductive complacency, even as our education, patriotism, and unity have declined.

claims.text: A decline in patriotism is the most dangerous consequence of the Romantic tradition in a democracy.
claims.source_passage: Nothing can be more dangerous in a democracy than a steep decline in patriotism.

claims.text: The romantic focus on individual identity and ethnicity clashes with the collectivist sentiment required for patriotism.
claims.source_passage: Such a collectivist sentiment clashes with the individualistic flavor of romantic child-centered education.

claims.text: American schools are responsible for perpetuating individualistic developments focused on feelings, happiness, and narrow ethnic identity.
claims.source_passage: Our schools bear responsibility for perpetuating these individualistic developments, which become self-perpetuating as school graduates become teachers.

claims.text: Schools must restore the 'fashionability' of patriotism to maintain the social contract.
claims.source_passage: Patriotism—the emotional side of the “social contract” celebrated by Hamilton and Jefferson—needs to get fashionable again.

claims.text: The Enlightenment social contract is a two-way obligation between the citizen and the nation, not a one-way indulgence of the individual's nature.
claims.source_passage: Educators need to grasp that America does not owe each child a special indulgence for her “nature.” The Enlightenment social contract is a two-way obligation.

claims.text: Patriotism represents the emotional dimension of the Enlightenment social contract.
claims.source_passage: Patriotism—the emotional side of the “social contract” celebrated by Hamilton and Jefferson—needs to get fashionable again.

claims.text: The nation-state is not being replaced by globalism.
claims.source_passage: the nation-state is not going away in favor of some vague Hegelian march towards globalism.

claims.text: America does not owe children special indulgence for their 'nature' because the social contract is a two-way obligation.
claims.source_passage: Educators need to grasp that America does not owe each child a special indulgence for her “nature.” The Enlightenment social contract is a two-way obligation.

claims.text: State politicians have a constitutional obligation to educate the children within their state.
claims.source_passage: They have a constitutional obligation to educate the children of their state.

claims.text: State legislators must mandate grade-by-grade topic commonality to enable the 'Kim effect' of improved literacy.
claims.source_passage: As legislators, they need to mandate enough grade-by-grade topic commonality to enable the Kim effect to take place.

claims.text: High national literacy can only be achieved through a curriculum that results in shared, grapholectic knowledge.
claims.source_passage: That can only be done by a well-thought-out, enabling curriculum that results in shared, grapholectic knowledge.

claims.text: Interpretation of human speech—whether by children or AI—requires silently shared background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: If artificial intelligence (AI) has to gain the silently shared background knowledge well enough to master the interpretation of human speech, so must a young American child.

claims.text: The decline in American unity and verbal scores was caused by wrong educational ideas and can be reversed by right ones.
claims.source_passage: If our unity and our verbal curve have gone down through wrong ideas, they can be brought back up by right ones.

claims.text: Child-centered education contains an implicit 'predestination theology' regarding a child's innate ideas.
claims.source_passage: In his blank-slate conception of the child’s mind, John Locke had rightly attacked any predestination theology of the kind that is implicit in child-centered education.

claims.text: Progress in science and technology does not guarantee automatic progress in social and educational theory.
claims.source_passage: The romantic faith in an ongoing, unfolding historical progress, while vaguely true of science and technology... is not the same as automatic progress in social and educational theory.

claims.text: Uncritical faith in progress leads to a complacent arrogance and a sense of moral superiority over previous generations.
claims.source_passage: The assumption that time and nature are marching beneficently onward leads paradoxically to a complacent arrogance regarding one’s own virtue and superiority.

claims.text: Bi-culturalism, defined as possessing the shared knowledge of the national grapholect alongside a home culture, is essential to modern social health.
claims.source_passage: In any case, bi-culturalism, in which one culture is the shared knowledge of the national grapholect, is essential to social health and national cohesion in the modern world.

claims.text: Nations based on shared background knowledge are the most durable and potentially fair form of social organization.
claims.source_passage: Nations with their armies and navies and their grapholects, based on shared background knowledge, have proved to be the most durable and potentially fair form of human social organization.

claims.text: History indicates that multi-lingual empires are less successful than unified nations.
claims.source_passage: History has not been kind to multi-lingual empires.

claims.text: Romantic notions of the child's innate ideas and identities are nation-weakening and democracy-weakening mistakes.
claims.source_passage: That has been a nation-weakening, democracy-weakening mistake.

claims.text: Nations can work both technically and emotionally to provide a stable framework for citizens.
claims.source_passage: But nations can work technically and emotionally.

claims.text: Patriotism and high literacy are ideals that remain worth advancing and teaching in schools.
claims.source_passage: And while that continues, patriotism and high literacy continue to be ideals worth advancing and teaching.

claims.text: The American form of the democratic nation-state is highly successful so long as it avoids romantic educational and identity-based ideologies.
claims.source_passage: The American form of the democratic nation has proved to be a highly successful form of the nation-state—so long as it stops tearing itself apart with romantic notions of the child and her innate ideas and her innate identities.

claims.text: Educational mistakes caused by Romanticism can be corrected by adopting a form of schooling that uses a common national grapholect.
claims.source_passage: The mistake can be corrected by adopting a sounder form of schooling that embraces and enables everybody by means of a common national grapholect.

claims.text: High mastery of the national grapholect should be the possession of every citizen.
claims.source_passage: It makes high mastery of the national grapholect the possession of everyone

claims.text: Mastery of a national grapholect encourages rather than suppresses the preservation of home ethnicities.
claims.source_passage: but at the same time encourages any home ethnicity anybody chooses to preserve.

claims.text: Bi-ethnicity unified by shared nationality and patriotism is a modern and necessary social structure.
claims.source_passage: Bi-ethnicity unified by shared nationality and patriotism are as up-to-date today as they were at the dawn of literate modernity.

claims.text: Adhering to Enlightenment political conceptions provides the best defense against the challenges of AI and post-modernism.
claims.source_passage: We will be better able to deal with the challenges of AI and “post-modernism” if we should stick with the political conceptions that created modern democracies.

claims.text: Romantic educational theory is deeply wrong despite its status as poetry.
claims.source_passage: The Romantics were wonderful poets but deeply wrong educational theorists.

claims.text: Ethnicities are constructed and learned rather than innate at birth.
claims.source_passage: Ethnicities are made not born. So rightly said John Locke and our founders.

claims.text: American literacy scores declined gradually after the influence of Hegel and the 'religion of nature' clouded Enlightenment notions.
claims.source_passage: After that notion got clouded by the post-enlightenment religion of nature, with Hegel being its high priest, our literacy scores gradually went downhill.

claims.text: Shared background knowledge instruction enables success even for children born into poverty and diverse cultures.
claims.source_passage: It has produced smart, literate, and patriotic citizens—including these children in the South Bronx who were born indigent and of diverse cultures and races.

claims.text: While educational improvement cannot occur overnight, it can be achieved relatively quickly if individual states act as successful models that inspire citizens in other states to demand similar reforms.
claims.source_passage: Certainly, that cannot be done overnight. Yet it can be done pretty fast by individual states aiming to become happier and better places. Their success will then encourage citizens of other states to demand educational improvement.

claims.text: The religious impulse in humans is persistent; when traditional 'revealed religion' was sidelined by the Enlightenment, it re-emerged as a new 'earthly religion' celebrating Nature and natural instincts.
claims.source_passage: But the religious impulse in people doesn’t go away. A new earthly religion emerged in Europe and America not long after our Constitution was ratified... The new earthly religion celebrated Nature and natural instincts.

claims.text: Incoherent or 'tokenistic' multicultural schooling is suboptimal because it fails to impart the expertise in the national grapholect necessary for high literacy.
claims.source_passage: Neither can incoherent, tokenistic, multicultural schooling be optimal, that fails to impart expertise in the national grapholect. To impart that expertise is the primary duty of a nation’s schools in the modern era.

claims.text: State politicians must identify as 'ethnic Americans' as well as their partisan identities (Democrats or Republicans) to fulfill their constitutional obligation to educate children.
claims.source_passage: They need to start identifying themselves as ethnic Americans as well as ethnic Democrats and ethnic Republicans. As legislators, they need to mandate enough grade-by-grade topic commonality to enable the Kim effect to take place.

claims.text: The Romantic concept of the 'meddling intellect' posits that analytical reasoning 'murders' and 'dissects' the beautiful, unified forms of nature rather than understanding them intuitively.
claims.source_passage: Our meddling intellect / Mis-shapes the beauteous forms of things:— / We murder to dissect.

claims.text: Bi-ethnicity, defined as the combination of home culture and the culture of the national grapholect, is an inherent and permanent condition of modernity rather than a temporary transitional phase for immigrants.
claims.source_passage: And in fact, our 'first-generation' Americans often considered bi-ethnicity to be a temporary condition. But it need not be... Bi-ethnicity, the ethnicity of the home plus the ethnicity of the grapholect is (like literacy itself) an inherent condition of modernity.

claims.text: The current social and educational era is characterized by a loss of conviction among the 'best' and a 'passionate intensity' among the 'worst,' leading to social instability.
claims.source_passage: Yeats described our current era (in his ironic “The Second Coming”) this way: “The best lack all conviction, and the worst are full of passionate intensity.”

claims.text: The Hegel-Darwin-Dewey tradition fosters a sense of 'generational superiority,' where people believe they are intrinsically smarter and better than their predecessors simply because they exist later in time.
claims.source_passage: That, in turn, can lead to a sense of generational superiority, resentment, and a decline in patriotism... too many Americans think: “Since time marches on, we inheritors of all this progress, we Americans are also marching forward in mind and sentiment. Hence, we are intrinsically smarter and better than those who came before.”

claims.text: The Enlightenment social contract is a 'two-way obligation' that requires citizens to perform social duties for the nation, contradicting the Romantic view that the nation owes children special indulgence for their 'nature'.
claims.source_passage: Educators need to grasp that America does not owe each child a special indulgence for her “nature.” The Enlightenment social contract is a two-way obligation. John F. Kennedy said memorably: “Ask not what your country can do for you; Ask what you can do for your country.”

claims.text: Hegel’s concept of 'Geist' served as the intellectual manifestation of 'Holy Nature,' providing a theological justification for the belief that social progress is a divinely directed necessity.
claims.source_passage: Geist was the intellectual manifestation of Holy Nature. With such Divinely directed moral progress, the improvement of society could be taken for granted.

evidence.description: PISA rankings showing the US at 25th in elementary school quality.
Professor Hayes's chart showing the decline of mean verbal scores from 1957 to 1992.
The 'French Elbow' chart showing the impact of early schooling on disadvantaged students.
The March of Time newsreel 'Education in the 1940s' featuring John Dewey.
Historical account of the Thirty Years War killing a third of Germany's population, leading to Enlightenment rejection of sectarian theology.
Wordsworth's 'Lyrical Ballads' and specific poems like 'My Heart Leaps Up' and 'The Tables Turned' are used to illustrate the Romantic view of 'natural piety' and the child.
William Blake's poem 'The Garden of Love' is cited to show the post-Revolutionary rejection of institutional religion in favor of nature.
Richard Rorty's characterization of John Dewey's work as a combination of Hegel and Darwin.
Hegel's 'The Phenomenology of Spirit' (1807) is cited as the primary text influencing Dewey's belief in inevitable historical progress.
Historical milestones of progress attributed to Enlightenment activism rather than Romanticism.
Jill Lepore's argument in 'This America' regarding the persistence of the nation-state.
John F. Kennedy's 1961 inaugural address emphasizing social duty over individual entitlement.
The requirement for Artificial Intelligence to possess shared background knowledge in order to interpret human speech.
John Locke's blank-slate philosophy as a rebuttal to the idea of innate ideas or predestination.
Ernest Gellner's research on the relationship between modernity and the development of national grapholects.
The historical American tradition of 'bi-ethnicity' exemplified by terms like 'Polish-American' or 'Chinese-American'.
The success of middle-school debaters from the South Bronx who used the Core Knowledge Sequence to win city-wide trophies despite coming from indigent and diverse backgrounds.

concepts.author_definition: A false belief, rooted in Romanticism, that Nature will guide a child's instincts and development correctly without the need for shared communal knowledge.
concepts.source_passage: In this final chapter I will briefly describe how that trust in nature has deluded us and caused our current second-rate educational outcomes... That trust in Nature is still a theme in American life and American education.

concepts.author_definition: The standard written language of a nation that schools are obligated to teach to ensure fairness and literacy.
concepts.source_passage: Neither can incoherent, tokenistic, multicultural schooling be optimal, that fails to impart expertise in the national grapholect. To impart that expertise is the primary duty of a nation’s schools in the modern era.

concepts.author_definition: A European frame of mind post-French Revolution that rejects Enlightenment reason in favor of nature-worship and individualism.
concepts.source_passage: That religion of nature was a product of the general European frame of mind that developed after the French Revolution... “romanticism” infused the United States in figures like Emerson, Thoreau, Walt Whitman and later John Dewey.

concepts.author_definition: The intellectual manifestation of 'Holy Nature' or a world spirit that evolves through history as a necessary spiritual dimension of nature.
The Lockean concept that children are born without inborn ideas, making them equal at birth and necessitating education as formation for citizenship.
concepts.common_alternative: The Romantic view that children are born with holy, untainted instincts.
concepts.source_passage: Our founders’ common school conceived the child as being born with a “blank slate” (John Locke’s term) without any inborn ideas from “nature,” a word that means “inborn.”

concepts.author_definition: The Wordsworthian practice of celebrating and following the holy, untainted instincts of the young child.
A term from Wordsworth used by the author to characterize the complacent, non-interventionist stance of Romanticism in education.
concepts.common_alternative: A poetic state of receptive meditation.

concepts.author_definition: A social sentiment and collectivist willingness to sacrifice personal benefit for the nation; the emotional side of the social contract.
concepts.common_alternative: Often viewed by modern educators as unfashionable or individualistic identity-based.

concepts.author_definition: A two-way obligation between citizen and state requiring active performance of social duties, as defined by Enlightenment thinkers.
The dual identity consisting of the ethnicity of the home culture plus the 'ethnicity' of the national grapholect (shared national knowledge).
concepts.common_alternative: Often seen as a temporary transitional state or as 'multiculturalism' which may de-emphasize the national component.

concepts.author_definition: The implicit belief in child-centered pedagogy that a child's nature or innate identity is fixed and must be followed rather than shaped by a shared curriculum.
A Romantic pejorative for analytical, logical reasoning that supposedly destroys the intuitive, organic unity of nature by 'dissecting' it.
The German philosophical equivalent of Wordsworth’s 'Nature,' used by Schelling and Hegel to describe a divine spiritual entity unfolding through history.
An incoherent form of schooling that focuses on fragmented ethnic identities at the expense of imparting the shared national grapholect necessary for true literacy.
A primary national identity based on shared language and civic duty that the author argues should transcend partisan or narrow ancestral identities.
In the Hegelian sense, the account of human history as a necessary and spiritual unfolding of thought (Geist) governed by divine logic.

cases.name: The March of Time newsreel (1940s)
cases.description: A popular newsreel and radio program from the 1930s-40s that reflected American optimism. It featured John Dewey advocating for progressive, child-centered education, framing it as preparing children for an unpredictable future.
key_passages: The aging John Dewey (1859-1942) was featured in more than one March of Time newsreel. One of them was entitled “Education in the 1940s.” It was devoted to the new romantic child-centered, “progressive” education.
We must prepare our children not for the world of the past, not for our world, but for their world.

cases.name: The US 80-year educational experiment
cases.description: The period from roughly the 1940s to the present where American education shifted from Enlightenment-based common schooling to Romantic, child-centered progressive education.
key_passages: Our 80-year educational experiment with Romantic faith is a failed experiment—as Professor Hayes graphically showed in his chart of our verbal scores.

cases.name: The American Common School
cases.description: The early educational institution of the United States, which the author claims was based on Lockean principles of the 'blank slate.' It aimed to form children into happy, effective members of the nation through common knowledge.
key_passages: Our founders’ common school conceived the child as being born with a “blank slate” (John Locke’s term) without any inborn ideas from “nature,” a word that means “inborn.”
contestable_aspects: The extent to which all founders actually adhered to a strict Lockean blank-slate view in educational practice.

cases.name: The French Revolution (1789)
cases.description: A pivotal historical event that, along with the American Revolution, emboldened thinkers to reject traditional religious dogmas and look toward 'Nature' and 'Geist' for spiritual fulfillment.
key_passages: The French Revolution (key date: 1789) along with the American Revolution (Constitution ratified 1791) emboldened romantic thinkers to look not for pie in the sky, but to pie on earth

cases.name: The American Enlightenment
cases.description: The period of Locke, Hamilton, Jefferson, and later Lincoln, characterized by activist ideas, eternal vigilance, and the pursuit of abolition and equality through rational effort rather than assuming inevitable progress.
key_passages: It was accomplished by the “meddling intellect” of Enlightenment figures like Locke and Hamilton and Jefferson and later—Lincoln.
They said: “The price of liberty is eternal vigilance,” not eternal complacency.

cases.name: South Bronx Core Knowledge Implementation
cases.description: A successful implementation of the Core Knowledge Sequence led by Jeff Litt in the South Bronx. Despite the students being born into poverty ('indigent') and coming from diverse cultures and races, the curriculum enabled them to become some of the best middle-school debaters in New York City and 'firmly bi-ethnic' citizens.
key_passages: It has produced smart, literate, and patriotic citizens—including these children in the South Bronx who were born indigent and of diverse cultures and races. Now they are firmly bi-ethnic—holding up their New-York-City-wide debating trophy! They are some of the best middle-school debaters in the City of New York.

thinkers.name: John Dewey
thinkers.key_idea: Children should be educated for 'their world' (the future) rather than the world of the past or present, using child-centered methods.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch critiques Dewey's premise as a non sequitur that relies on a false 'religion of nature' to bridge the gap between an unpredictable future and child-led development.
key_passages: We must prepare our children not for the world of the past, not for our world, but for their world.
That trust in Nature is still a theme in American life and American education. It is implicit in Dewey’s non sequitur.

thinkers.name: John Locke
thinkers.key_idea: Enlightenment philosopher whose ideas guided the successful 'common school' era of American education.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Locke as a representative of the superior Enlightenment-based approach that preceded the Romantic decline.
key_passages: Our elementary education used to be first-rate under the “common school,” and the guidance of ideas from Enlightenment philosophers like John Locke.

thinkers.name: Professor Hayes
thinkers.key_idea: Documented the decline of American verbal scores over the late 20th century.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Hayes's data as empirical proof that the Romantic experiment in education has failed.
key_passages: Our 80-year educational experiment with Romantic faith is a failed experiment—as Professor Hayes graphically showed in his chart of our verbal scores.

thinkers.name: William Wordsworth
thinkers.key_idea: Natural piety and the belief that the child is 'closer to the divine source' than the analytical adult.
thinkers.engagement: The author uses Wordsworth's poetry to illustrate the irrational 'religion of nature' that replaced rational educational aims.
key_passages: Wordsworth said we should follow “natural piety,” by celebrating the untainted, holy instincts of the young child.
The child was closer to the divine source than the grown up whose “meddling intellect” “dissected” things

thinkers.name: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
thinkers.key_idea: The inevitable, dialectical progress of the 'Geist' or world spirit through human history.
thinkers.engagement: The author identifies Hegel as the source of the 'progressive' doctrine that social improvement and educational growth are inevitable and divinely directed.
key_passages: Hegel’s notion of a gradually evolving world spirit marching ever forward in time led to an American change of educational thought
It proposed a story of mental development as part of an inevitable theological cosmic progress.

thinkers.name: G.W.F. Hegel
thinkers.key_idea: History is a necessary, spiritual unfolding (phenomenology) governed by an unstoppable 'Logik' of progress.
thinkers.engagement: The author identifies Hegel as the source of the 'complacent Romanticism' that infected American educational thought via Dewey.
key_passages: Hegel’s book encouraged Dewey to believe that “Time Marches On”—and is doing so inexorably

thinkers.name: Jill Lepore
thinkers.key_idea: The nation-state remains a necessary and durable form of human organization against the 'Hegelian march' of globalism.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Lepore's historical perspective to defend the importance of national identity and patriotism in education.
key_passages: schools begin to grasp Jill Lepore’s argument in her This America, that the nation-state is not going away in favor of some vague Hegelian march towards globalism.

thinkers.name: Ernest Gellner
thinkers.key_idea: Modernity is structurally congruent with the development of the nation-state and shared literate languages (grapholects).
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch cites Gellner to argue that nations with shared background knowledge are the most durable and fair forms of social organization in the modern world.
key_passages: Modernity, as historians like Ernest Gellner and Jill Lepore have shown, is congruent with nationality.

thinkers.name: W. B. Yeats
thinkers.key_idea: The modern era is characterized by a lack of conviction in the best and passionate intensity in the worst.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch uses Yeats' 'The Second Coming' to describe the regressive effects of uncritical faith in progress.
key_passages: Yeats described our current era (in his ironic “The Second Coming”) this way: “The best lack all conviction, and the worst are full of passionate intensity.”

thinkers.name: The Romantics
thinkers.key_idea: Educational theorists who prioritized passive nature-religion and the child's innate ideas over systematic knowledge.
thinkers.engagement: Hirsch praises them as poets but dismisses them as 'deeply wrong educational theorists' whose ideas have weakened democracy.
key_passages: The Romantics were wonderful poets but deeply wrong educational theorists.

actors.name: Time magazine
actors.context: Successful news publication that reflected and promoted early 20th-century American optimism.

actors.name: Supreme Court of the United States
actors.context: Ruled against affirmative action in June 2023, creating a contemporary exigency for Hirsch's reforms.

actors.name: PISA (Program for International Student Assessment)
actors.context: International body whose rankings Hirsch uses to demonstrate American educational failure.

actors.name: The American Founders
actors.context: Cited for their insistence on separation of church and state and the Lockean common school model.

actors.name: Enlightenment Thinkers
actors.context: Thinkers who grew suspicious of revealed religion due to the Wars of Religion.

actors.name: Richard Rorty
actors.context: Philosopher cited for his accurate description of Dewey's synthesis of Hegel and Darwin.

actors.name: Enlightenment Philosophers
actors.context: The group (Locke, Hamilton, Jefferson) whose activism actually achieved social progress.

actors.name: American Schools
actors.context: Institutions currently responsible for perpetuating individualistic and anti-patriotic sentiments.

actors.name: State Politicians
actors.context: Identified as the primary actors responsible for mandating common curricula to restore literacy.

actors.name: John F. Kennedy
actors.context: Cited to illustrate the concept of the social contract as a two-way obligation of duty to the country.

actors.name: Hamilton and Jefferson
actors.context: Cited as the original celebrates of the Enlightenment social contract and patriotism.

actors.name: Jeff Litt
actors.context: The educator responsible for implementing the Core Knowledge curriculum in the South Bronx case.

actors.name: American Founders
actors.context: Cited alongside Locke as proponents of the Enlightenment view that identities are made, not born.

objections_raised.objection: We must prepare children for their future world, not for our world or the world of the past, because the future is moving at a tremendous rate.
objections_raised.response: This logic is a non sequitur. If the future is unpredictable, focusing on a child's natural instincts does not logically prepare them for it unless one assumes Nature is a divine guide, which Hirsch argues is a false religion.

objections_raised.objection: Child-centered education is necessary to prepare the child for an 'unknown future.'
objections_raised.response: This confidence is a 'too-complacent' Hegelian religious doctrine; progress actually requires the vigilance and activism of Enlightenment principles, and the future is not 'unknown' to those who understand the requirements of the social contract.

objections_raised.objection: The nation-state is becoming obsolete in favor of globalism.
objections_raised.response: The nation-state remains the most durable and potentially fair form of social organization, and patriotism is necessary for its health.

objections_raised.objection: Schools should prioritize the individual child's 'nature' and innate identity.
objections_raised.response: This is a form of predestination theology that ignores the blank-slate reality of learning and the two-way obligation of the social contract.
